This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other members of the tribe. intro: Diodonopsis hoeijeri, also known as the Peruvian orchid, is a rare and endangered species of orchid native to the Andes mountain range in Peru. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Diodonopsis hoeijeri include flavonoids, terpenes, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Diodonopsis hoeijeri is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat respiratory conditions and promote overall health and well-being.
